title_id: Surat al-Nawawi tentang Kebun-kebun Rakyat Syam
content_en: They are the ones who are just with respect to rulings, people and what they are in charge of."4 [Then al-Nawawi praises the ruler for all the good that he had done. He asks Allah to continue his rule and to bless him .... ] Allah has made it obligatory to give 1 Here again, al-Nawawi is paraphrasing a hadith found in al-Bukhari and Muslim. 2 This text was recorded by Muslim. Actually, in al-Nawawi's letter he reversed the two sentences. 3 Recorded by al-Bukhari and Muslim. 4 This was recorded by Muslim. Again, al-Nawawi somewhat paraphrased the text. The Lifa of Imam al-Nawawi thanks for His blessings and He has promised to give more to those who are thankful. Allah has said, U /. .... ,,.. ,,. ,.. րց/'iֵ v; "If you give thanks, I will give you more" [Ibraaheem 7]. The Muslims have faced, due to this taking over of their property, such different forms of harm that no one could possibly put into words. You have sought from them proof that should not be required of them. None of the scholars of the Muslims have ever declared that land legal [for the rulers to take]. In fact, if someone possesses something, it is considered his property. It is not allowed to deny him or to require him to prove his ownership. The life of the Sultan is known to be of one who loves acting according to the Shariah. You advise your deputies to do such. So it is much more befitting that you also do so. You are responsible for freeing the people from this problem and solving all of their problems concerning it. If you free them, Allah will free you from every disliked deed, for these people are the weak, some of them are orphans, poor, weak and righteous people. It is through these people that you are aided, rescued and provided for. They are the inhabitants of the blessed land of Sham and the neighbors of the Prophets (peace be upon them). They are to be treated with due respect and should not be harmed for any reason. If the Sultan would see what hardship they are facing and their great grieving, he would release them immediately and would not delay. However, the matter is not being presented to him correctly. By Allah, rescue the Muslims and Allah will rescue you. Be kind with them and Allah will be kind with you. Bring them a solution soon before the rains come and they lose all their crops. Most of them inherited their lands from the previous generations and it is not possible for them to provide a script showing that they bought the land. If the Sultan is kind to them, then he has for him the supplication of the Prophet (peace be upon him) for those who are kind to his Ummah and he will also be helped against his enemies. Allah has said, "If you help (in the cause of) Allah, He will help you" [Muhammad 7]. The citizenry will also pray for you. Your dominion will be filled with blessings. It will be blessed in all of the good deeds you intend. There is a hadith from the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) that states, 4 1 Commentary on the Forty Hadith of al-Nawawi "Whoever establishes a good practice in Islam that is acted upon after him shall have recorded for him the reward of everyone who acted upon it without the reward of any of them being lessened. Whoever establishes an evil practice in Islam that is acted upon after him shall have recorded for him the burden of everyone who acted upon it without the burden of any of them being lessened."1 We ask Allah, the Noble, to guide the Sultan to a good deed by which he will be remembered until the Day of Judgment and to protect him from an evil practice. This is the advice to the Sultan that is obligatory upon us. We hope from Allah that Allah will inspire him to accept it. Peace be upon you and the mercy and blessings of Allah. All praises are due to Allah, the Lord of the Worlds. And the peace of blessings be upon our leader Muhammad, his family and his companions.2 Another narration states that the ruler was able to get a number of jurists, some out of fear and some for worldly benefit, to sign a fatwa allowing the ruler to do what he had decided upon. Some of the scholars refused to sign the decree. However, it is stated that the ruler had some of the scholars who did not sign the fatwa killed. 3 This seems a bit strange as it is somewhat out of line with what is known about al-Dhaahir Baibars, his relationship with the Muslim scholars and even with how al-Nawawi deals with him in his letters. Perhaps the stronger narration is the narration that states that the ruler became very upset with al-Nawawi after these encounters.
content_id:
Surat al-Nawawi kepada penguasa tentang kebun-kebun rakyat Syam dilanjutkan dengan pujian kepada sultan atas kebaikan-kebaikannya, disertai penguatan argumen hukum: Allah mewajibkan syukur atas nikmat-Nya dan berjanji akan menambahkannya: 'Jika kamu bersyukur, niscaya Aku akan menambah nikmat-Ku kepadamu' (Ibrahim: 7). Kaum Muslim telah mengalami berbagai bentuk kerugian akibat perampasan harta mereka yang tidak dapat dilukiskan dengan kata-kata. Engkau telah meminta dari mereka bukti yang seharusnya tidak perlu diminta. Tidak ada satu pun ulama Muslim yang pernah menyatakan tanah-tanah itu halal diambil oleh penguasa. Siapa pun yang memiliki sesuatu, itu adalah miliknya. Tidak diperbolehkan menyangkalnya atau memintanya untuk membuktikan kepemilikannya.

Kehidupan sultan dikenal sebagai seseorang yang mencintai amal sesuai syariat. Engkau memerintahkan wakilmu untuk melakukan hal yang demikian — maka lebih sepantasnya lagi engkau juga melakukannya. Engkau bertanggung jawab untuk membebaskan rakyat dari masalah ini. Jika engkau membebaskan mereka, Allah akan membebaskanmu dari setiap perbuatan yang tidak disukai. Mereka adalah penduduk tanah Syam yang diberkahi dan para tetangga para nabi. Jika sultan melihat langsung kesengsaraan dan kesedihan yang mereka rasakan, niscaya ia akan segera melepaskan mereka. Namun masalah ini tidak disampaikan kepadanya dengan benar. Demi Allah, selamatkanlah kaum Muslim dan Allah akan menyelamatkan engkau. Kebanyakan dari mereka mewarisi tanah dari generasi sebelumnya dan tidak mungkin bagi mereka untuk menyediakan surat bukti pembelian.

Jika sultan berbuat baik kepada mereka, ia akan mendapatkan doa Nabi bagi mereka yang berbuat baik kepada umatnya, serta pertolongan melawan musuh. Allah berfirman: 'Jika kamu menolong agama Allah, niscaya Dia akan menolongmu' (Muhammad: 7). Nabi juga bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang membuat sunnah hasanah (kebiasaan baik) dalam Islam yang diamalkan setelahnya, maka dicatat baginya pahalanya serta pahala orang-orang yang mengamalkannya tanpa mengurangi pahala mereka sedikit pun. Dan barangsiapa membuat sunnah sayyi'ah (kebiasaan buruk) dalam Islam, maka dicatat baginya dosanya serta dosa orang-orang yang mengamalkannya tanpa mengurangi dosa mereka sedikit pun.'

Disebutkan pula bahwa penguasa berhasil mendapatkan sejumlah fuqaha — sebagian karena takut dan sebagian karena kepentingan duniawi — untuk menandatangani fatwa yang membolehkan tindakan penguasa. Sebagian ulama menolak menandatanganinya. Diriwayatkan bahwa penguasa menjadi sangat marah kepada al-Nawawi setelah berbagai pertemuan ini — kemungkinan riwayat inilah yang lebih kuat.
content_ar: They are the ones who are just with respect to rulings, people and what they are in charge of."4 [Then النووي praises the ruler for all the good that he had done. He asks الله to continue his rule and to bless him .... ] الله has made it واجب to give 1 Here again, النووي is paraphrasing a الحديث found in البخاري and المسلم. 2 This text was recorded by المسلم. Actually, in النووي's letter he reversed the two sentences. 3 Recorded by البخاري and المسلم. 4 This was recorded by المسلم. Again, النووي somewhat paraphrased the text. The Lifa of الإمام النووي thanks for His blessings and He has promised to give more to those who are thankful. الله has said, U /. .... ,,.. ,,. ,.. րց/'iֵ v; "If you give thanks, I will give you more" [Ibraaheem 7]. The المسلمون have faced, due to this taking over of their property, such different forms of harm that no one could possibly put into words. You have sought from them proof that should not be required of them. None of the العلماء of the المسلمون have ever declared that land legal [for the rulers to take]. In fact, if someone possesses something, it is considered his property. It is not allowed to deny him or to require him to prove his ownership. The life of the Sultan is known to be of one who loves acting according to the الشريعة الإسلامية. You advise your deputies to do such. So it is much more befitting that you also do so. You are responsible for freeing the people from this problem and solving all of their problems concerning it. If you free them, الله will free you from every مكروه deed, for these people are the weak, some of them are orphans, poor, weak and righteous people. It is through these people that you are aided, rescued and provided for. They are the inhabitants of the blessed land of Sham and the neighbors of the Prophets (peace be upon them). They are to be treated with due respect and should not be harmed for any reason. If the Sultan would see what hardship they are facing and their great grieving, he would release them immediately and would not delay. However, the matter is not being presented to him correctly. By الله, rescue the المسلمون and الله will rescue you. Be kind with them and الله will be kind with you. Bring them a solution soon before the rains come and they lose all their crops. Most of them inherited their lands from the previous generations and it is not possible for them to provide a script showing that they bought the land. If the Sultan is kind to them, then he has for him the الدعاء of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) for those who are kind to his الأمة الإسلامية and he will also be helped against his enemies. الله has said, "If you help (in the cause of) الله, He will help you" [Muhammad 7]. The citizenry will also pray for you. Your dominion will be filled with blessings. It will be blessed in all of the الأعمال الصالحة you intend. There is a الحديث from رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) that states, 4 1 Commentary on the Forty الحديث of النووي "Whoever establishes a good practice in الإسلام that is acted upon after him shall have recorded for him the reward of everyone who acted upon it without the reward of any of them being lessened. Whoever establishes an evil practice in الإسلام that is acted upon after him shall have recorded for him the burden of everyone who acted upon it without the burden of any of them being lessened."1 We ask الله, the Noble, to guide the Sultan to a good deed by which he will be remembered until the يوم القيامة and to protect him from an evil practice. This is the advice to the Sultan that is واجب upon us. We hope from الله that الله will inspire him to accept it. Peace be upon you and the mercy and blessings of الله. All praises are due to الله, the الرب of the Worlds. And the peace of blessings be upon our leader Muhammad, his family and his الصحابة.2 Another روى states that the ruler was able to get a number of الفقهاء, some out of fear and some for worldly benefit, to sign a الفتوى allowing the ruler to do what he had decided upon. Some of the العلماء refused to sign the decree. However, it is stated that the ruler had some of the العلماء who did not sign the الفتوى killed. 3 This seems a bit strange as it is somewhat out of line with what is known about al-Dhaahir Baibars, his relationship with the المسلم العلماء and even with how النووي deals with him in his letters. Perhaps the stronger روى is the روى that states that the ruler became very upset with النووي after these encounters.
